label: Conclave project intro
excerpt: Conclave is described as a confidential layer for a cohort, deriving signals from participant-local data.

label: agentic map discussion
excerpt: Conclave, context, and router were called out as having strong synergy around input, configuration, and ingestion.

label: model-routing need
excerpt: Bitrouter was framed as a module for routing through frontier, private, or local inference without coupling to one provider.

label: JJHub definition
excerpt: JJHub was described as a GitHub-like system with a large AI-built test corpus, still in private alpha.

label: confidential data stack
excerpt: TeeSQL was described as hosting Postgres high-availability clusters inside confidential virtual machines.

label: privacy-stack synergy
excerpt: TeeSQL, router, and SignalStack were discussed as complementary pieces of a privacy-oriented stack.

label: SignalStack intro
excerpt: SignalStack was presented as privacy-preserving AI services for Signal, including translation and group-chat tools.

label: TEE substrate discussion
excerpt: The TEE discussion notes multiple teams reinventing similar DStack, measurement, and attested TLS pieces.

label: Elocute project intro
excerpt: Elocute was introduced as a private AI speaking-practice system for short articulation sessions and feedback.

label: PMF workshop context
excerpt: The PMF workshop emphasized retention, payment, and repeatable customer segments over broad narrative appeal.

label: prediction-market hedging agent
excerpt: Presented an agent that decomposes a portfolio into risk factors, selects matching prediction-market events via simulation, and executes hedges through an RFQ layer with protected order flow discussed for the settlement leg.

label: behavior modulation via agents
excerpt: Presented a system treating the user as a black box steered by screen-capture inputs and rubric-scored notifications, with encrypted friend status sharing and attested-enclave upgrade approvals as the privacy backbone.

label: live conversation visualizer
excerpt: Presented a real-time speech-to-visual stage system with evidence that live visualization raises audience engagement, plus its inference, compositing, and self-improving shader toolchain.

label: cohort calendar bot in TEE
excerpt: The cohort's Matrix calendar bot was mentioned as one of the dev-mode sub-apps running inside the multiplexed dstack web-host CVM, a candidate for live promotion to attested mode.

label: Derive-then-destroy quantum-safe identity
excerpt: Verified ID data becomes one-time entropy for deterministic post-quantum keys, leaving only an on-chain commitment after the source data is erased.

label: Signature-delegated sovereign data spaces
excerpt: A user key creates a data space and delegates scoped capabilities down a signed chain so apps, backends, and agents all act under the owner's authority.

label: Dev-proof Postgres inside enclaves
excerpt: A high-availability Postgres cluster in confidential VMs uses attestation-gated membership and on-chain control signals so even the operators provably cannot touch raw data.

label: Enclave AI bots for messengers
excerpt: Translation, transcription, and chat bots run inference inside TEEs so adding AI to an encrypted messenger does not reintroduce a plaintext middlebox.

label: agent outreach CRM demo
excerpt: A kanban-style outreach tool with contact enrichment, email verification, and agent-drafted sends was demoed and offered to cohort teams with outreach needs.

label: coordinator-of-agents cluster workflow
excerpt: A coordinator model spawns, monitors, and cross-reviews worker agents across git worktrees for cluster development, including catching hallucinated on-chain spending.

label: router as sole MCP
excerpt: One presenter noted the cohort's router was the only MCP wired into an otherwise vanilla coding-agent setup, used for routing posts to the shared channel.

label: agent context indexer intro
excerpt: Introduced a context engine that clusters agent episodes into a hierarchical mindmap and deterministically re-injects only the currently relevant subtree.

label: confidential cohort signal layer
excerpt: Pitched a TEE-backed always-on agent that mines participants' private logs and surfaces only derived signals to cohort organizers.

label: ambient work-sharing MCP router
excerpt: Demoed an MCP server whose dynamic tool description turns coding sessions into scoped feed posts and surfaces relevant peers' work in-session.

label: durable React workflow orchestration
excerpt: Walked through a durable orchestrator that renders agent workflows as React trees with crash recovery, review loops, and time-travel debugging.

label: Confidential Postgres complements data substrate
excerpt: The data-sovereignty platform stores user-owned data in Postgres but deliberately drew its scope boundary before confidential hosting, making the TEE-hosted high-availability Postgres clusters an immediate, mutually acknowledged complement.

label: Sovereign inference routing module
excerpt: The application-layer team identified inference routing as a missing module in its stack, wanting to decouple its agents from any single provider and route progressively toward more private, local, and sovereign models.

label: IP anonymization meets model routing
excerpt: Offering the network-level IP-anonymization work as an add-on to the inference router was floated as a natural pairing, sharpening the open question of whether the router's edge is cheaper inference, private inference, or both.

label: End-to-end agentic pipeline
excerpt: Participants observed these three projects chain naturally into a single end-user product spanning configuration and input, long-horizon context ingestion, and serendipity-surfacing output delivery — the agentic version of the human connector role in a community.

label: privacy stack integration synergies
excerpt: TinyCloud identified TEE-hosted Postgres and model-routing projects as natural complements worth integrating rather than rebuilding in-house, reaffirming its choice to draw clear scope boundaries and lean on cohort neighbors.

label: agentic OS full-stack demo
excerpt: Shaw presented an open-source agentic operating system spanning local and cloud models, role-gated multi-user security, cross-platform identity graphs, and continuously learning robotics, all published in one repository designed for others to fork and build businesses on.

label: workflow engine fit signal
excerpt: The team doubled down on its agent workflow engine after users loudly demanded fixes when things broke instead of silently churning, reading that frustration as an early product-fit signal, while framing its agent-native code platform as a settlement layer that complements rather than replaces existing hosting.

label: friction as product philosophy
excerpt: The presenter argued that deliberately adding friction back into a consumer app is the point — private, slightly effortful daily speaking practice builds the habit that frictionless feeds erode.

label: TEE privacy as premium tier
excerpt: Cohort members suggested that a TEE-backed private mode could be the version users would pay a premium for, given how personal speaking-practice data can get.

label: policy-encumbered cross-chain exchange
excerpt: The Crossroads intro framed a cross-chain exchange where the signing key itself enforces policy, so withdrawals execute only when verified chain state satisfies encoded conditions rather than relying on a bridge operator or validator set.

label: solver economics reality check
excerpt: Mentor feedback pressed the team to validate whether cross-chain swap infrastructure is a viable market right now, citing thin solver margins across the industry and pointing toward agentic payments and coordination as a less crowded direction.

label: cross-team interview practice
excerpt: The presenter credited time spent supporting another cohort team's user-interview research with building the interviewing muscle and customer empathy he now applies to his own segment decisions.

label: live demo feedback loop
excerpt: Cohort members workshopped concrete legibility fixes for a voice-driven visual demo, including always-on captions and a seeded opening performance to lower the barrier for first participants.

label: RA-TLS inside database stack
excerpt: The TeeSQL design already performs an RA-TLS handshake between the CVM and the database leader, making it a natural early adopter for the proposed attested-TLS replacement layer.

label: on-chain CVM cluster membership
excerpt: A cohort builder demonstrated a cluster-membership contract that distributes shared encryption and WireGuard keys over blockchain messaging so CVMs self-assemble into an authenticated peer network beneath any service-mesh layer.

label: agent OS as Debian fork
excerpt: The team is building an agent operating system as a minimal Debian derivative, using Tails as a reference, and was pointed to mkosi to avoid maintaining a full fork.

label: router onboarding pipeline
excerpt: Walked the cohort through linking agents to the router, an agent-drafted self-introduction notebook, and Matrix account linking via a code handshake.

label: feed consumable by the OS
excerpt: The router exposes a simple REST API so the Shape Rotator OS can consume the shared notebook feed directly.

label: agentic interface checkpoint
excerpt: A checkpoint workshop on the interface and workflow layer — what people would use daily — framing self-optimizing, directable workflow runners as the real product surface.
